title: Minimal MCP Trio Runtime Governance Decision
objective: Decide whether the admitted minimal MCP trio should become part of default durable-host readiness or remain operator-on-demand now that the standalone repo cutover, operator-surface hardening, host parity repair, and clean host bootstrap proof are complete.
dependency_note: The operator-surface hardening lane is now published and mirrored cleanly on the authoritative host, and the current status board names the runtime-governance question as the next bounded Olares lane. The smallest truthful follow-on is a decision-only packet rather than a runtime mutation packet because the host is clean, the wrappers are published, and no concrete insufficiency currently requires always-on trio service.
decision_requirements: Prefer the lower-variance operator-on-demand boundary unless a concrete insufficiency is proven.
Do not treat published wrappers, host proof, or clean host parity as evidence that the trio must run continuously.
Do not admit broader AI services, ai_tasks, wrapper-level Codex integration, or unattended orchestration rollout by implication.
Keep GitHub canonical, `/home/olares/code/apex/apex-power-ops-platform` authoritative, and `/home/olares/src/apex-power-ops-platform` observe-only.
Name exactly one later packet candidate.
decision_rationale: The authoritative host bootstrap packet `2026-05-07-pre-runtime-decision` reported clean mirror head `7f64c651a6b17f1aef2867d643d755b39ea2425b` with `minimal_mcp.status = not-running`, and that posture is operationally coherent rather than degraded.
The admitted operator surface already supports bounded managed and adopted modes, so operators can start the trio when needed without widening the default host baseline.

No current packet, validation surface, or business lane requires the trio to remain continuously online between bounded sessions; current friction is governance and discoverability residue, not trio startup absence.

Keeping the trio operator-on-demand preserves the published lower-variance boundary while leaving room for a later durable-runtime packet if unattended workflow or repeated operator evidence makes that necessary.
selected_next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 096 - Historical Parent-Root Helper And Task Demotion Planning
selected_next_packet_scope: Inventory the highest-risk historical parent-root helper, task, and routing surfaces that can still misdirect delegated execution after the standalone repo cutover.
Classify each surface as preserve-as-provenance, relabel-as-historical, or replace-with-repo-root routing.
Select one smallest demotion or discoverability slice without reopening runtime or hosting scope.
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-07 after the operator-surface hardening lane was published, the `.tmp` residue was normalized, the status board was updated, and the authoritative host mirror returned to clean parity. Packet 095 keeps the admitted minimal MCP trio operator-on-demand by default and rejects default-runtime widening absent concrete insufficiency.
next_action: Update the current authority, runbook, cockpit, and status surfaces to reflect the operator-on-demand runtime verdict, publish the bounded doc packet, and then route the next lane to historical parent-root helper and task demotion planning.
